message:
test(checkpoint,context): improve export zip and compaction trigger coverage (bd-6cd0b)

Checkpoint export:
- exportZip: 52.9% → 79.4% (scrollback file with secret redaction path)

Context trigger:
- generateRecoveryPrompt: 62.5% → 100% (all 7 agent types including default)
- checkAllAgents: 60.0% → 80.0% (AutoCompact disabled + empty agents paths)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-3b3f","title":"Unit Tests: TUI rendering correctness and regression prevention","description":"## Overview
Comprehensive unit test suite for TUI rendering to prevent regressions and verify fixes for UI glitches.

## Test File Location
internal/tui/dashboard/dashboard_render_test.go

## Test Categories

### 1. View Height Management Tests
\`\`\`go
func TestView_OutputFitsTerminal(t *testing.T) {
    sizes := []struct{ w, h int }{
        {80, 24},   // Minimum
        {120, 40},  // Standard
        {200, 60},  // Large
    }
    for _, sz := range sizes {
        t.Run(fmt.Sprintf("%dx%d", sz.w, sz.h), func(t *testing.T) {
            m := NewModel(testSession)
            m, _ = m.Update(tea.WindowSizeMsg{Width: sz.w, Height: sz.h})
            
            view := m.View()
            lines := strings.Split(view, "\n")
            
            if len(lines) > sz.h {
                t.Errorf("view has %d lines, terminal height is %d", len(lines), sz.h)
            }
        })
    }
}
\`\`\`

### 2. Single-Render Tests (No Duplication)
\`\`\`go
func TestView_StatusBarRendersOnce(t *testing.T) {
    m := modelWithPanes(5)
    view := m.View()
    
    count := strings.Count(view, "Fleet:")
    if count != 1 {
        t.Errorf("expected 1 'Fleet:', got %d", count)
    }
}

func TestView_HelpBarRendersOnce(t *testing.T) {
    m := modelWithPanes(5)
    view := m.View()
    
    count := strings.Count(view, "navigate")
    if count != 1 {
        t.Errorf("expected 1 'navigate', got %d", count)
    }
}

func TestView_NoDuplicationOnResize(t *testing.T) {
    m := modelWithPanes(5)
    
    // Resize multiple times
    for _, h := range []int{24, 40, 20, 60, 30} {
        m, _ = m.Update(tea.WindowSizeMsg{Width: 120, Height: h})
        view := m.View()
        
        if strings.Count(view, "Fleet:") != 1 {
            t.Errorf("height %d: status bar duplicated", h)
        }
    }
}
\`\`\`

### 3. Width Calculation Tests
\`\`\`go
func TestView_WidthCalculation(t *testing.T) {
    // Verify we use lipgloss.Width not len()
    styled := lipgloss.NewStyle().Bold(true).Foreground(lipgloss.Color("212")).Render("test")
    
    if lipgloss.Width(styled) != 4 {
        t.Errorf("styled 'test' should have visual width 4, got %d", lipgloss.Width(styled))
    }
    
    if len(styled) <= 4 {
        t.Error("styled string should be longer than 4 bytes (includes ANSI)")
    }
}

func TestHelpBar_WidthTruncation(t *testing.T) {
    hints := []components.KeyHint{
        {Key: "↑↓", Desc: "navigate"},
        {Key: "1-9", Desc: "select"},
        {Key: "z", Desc: "zoom"},
        {Key: "r", Desc: "refresh"},
        {Key: "?", Desc: "help"},
        {Key: "q", Desc: "quit"},
    }
    
    // Narrow width should truncate
    narrow := components.RenderHelpBar(components.HelpBarOptions{
        Hints: hints,
        Width: 40,
    })
    
    // Wide width should show all
    wide := components.RenderHelpBar(components.HelpBarOptions{
        Hints: hints,
        Width: 200,
    })
    
    if lipgloss.Width(narrow) > 40 {
        t.Errorf("narrow help bar exceeds width: %d > 40", lipgloss.Width(narrow))
    }
    
    if !strings.Contains(wide, "quit") {
        t.Error("wide help bar should contain 'quit'")
    }
}
\`\`\`

### 4. List Deduplication Tests
\`\`\`go
func TestActivityPanel_NoDuplicates(t *testing.T) {
    panel := panels.NewActivityPanel()
    
    // Add activities with duplicates
    activities := []Activity{
        {ID: "1", Title: "Task A"},
        {ID: "1", Title: "Task A"}, // Duplicate
        {ID: "2", Title: "Task B"},
    }
    
    panel.SetData(activities)
    view := panel.View()
    
    if strings.Count(view, "Task A") != 1 {
        t.Error("duplicate 'Task A' found in view")
    }
}
\`\`\`

### 5. State Consistency Tests
\`\`\`go
func TestModel_FleetCountConsistency(t *testing.T) {
    m := modelWithPanes(10)
    m.claudeCount = 5
    m.codexCount = 3
    m.geminiCount = 2
    
    // Total should match
    total := m.claudeCount + m.codexCount + m.geminiCount
    if total != len(m.panes) {
        t.Errorf("agent counts (%d) != pane count (%d)", total, len(m.panes))
    }
}
\`\`\`

### 6. Golden File Tests
\`\`\`go
func TestView_GoldenRender(t *testing.T) {
    m := fixtureModel()
    m, _ = m.Update(tea.WindowSizeMsg{Width: 120, Height: 40})
    
    got := m.View()
    golden := filepath.Join("testdata", "dashboard_120x40.golden")
    
    if os.Getenv("UPDATE_GOLDEN") != "" {
        os.WriteFile(golden, []byte(got), 0644)
        t.Skip("golden file updated")
    }
    
    want, err := os.ReadFile(golden)
    if err != nil {
        t.Fatalf("read golden: %v", err)
    }
    
    if got != string(want) {
        t.Error("render differs from golden - run with UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 to update")
        // Write diff for debugging
        os.WriteFile("testdata/dashboard_120x40.got", []byte(got), 0644)
    }
}
\`\`\`

## Logging in Tests
All tests should use t.Log for detailed output:
\`\`\`go
t.Logf("Terminal size: %dx%d", width, height)
t.Logf("View lines: %d", len(lines))
t.Logf("Status bar count: %d", statusCount)
\`\`\`

## Test Data Fixtures
Create testdata/fixtures.go with:
- fixtureModel() - standard model with realistic data
- modelWithPanes(n) - model with n panes
- modelWithStatus(statuses) - model with specific statuses

## Coverage Requirements
- Minimum 80% coverage on dashboard.go View()
- 100% coverage on components/help.go RenderHelpBar()
- 90% coverage on layout calculations

## CI Integration
\`\`\`yaml
test-tui:
  runs-on: ubuntu-latest
  steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    - run: go test -v -race -coverprofile=coverage.out ./internal/tui/...
    - run: go tool cover -func=coverage.out | grep -E "View|Render"
\`\`\`","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:13:03.961681662Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T09:17:53.834265196Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T09:17:53.834192059Z","close_reason":"Added TUI rendering unit tests: TestView_NoDuplicationOnResize, TestLipglossWidth_VsLen, TestView_MultipleTerminalSizes. All 75+ dashboard tests passing.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3b3f","depends_on_id":"bd-1ay2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3b3f","depends_on_id":"bd-1xoe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3b3f","depends_on_id":"bd-36he","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3b3f","depends_on_id":"bd-3v69","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3b4yu","title":"Add ntm assign --watch for continuous auto-assignment on completion","description":"## Overview
Continuous auto-assignment mode that watches for completions and assigns new work automatically.

## Use Cases
1. "Fire and forget" swarm operation
2. Long-running development sessions
3. Overnight batch processing

## Usage
```bash
# Watch and auto-assign with default strategy
ntm assign myproject --watch

# With specific strategy
ntm assign myproject --watch --strategy=dependency

# With limit per cycle
ntm assign myproject --watch --limit=2

# Stop when no more beads
ntm assign myproject --watch --stop-when-done

# With delay between assignments
ntm assign myproject --watch --delay=5s
```

## Architecture
```go
type WatchLoop struct {
    session         string
    strategy        Strategy
    store           *AssignmentStore
    detector        *CompletionDetector
    depAssigner     *DependencyAwareAssigner
    
    // Concurrency control
    completionCh    chan CompletionEvent
    assignmentLock  sync.Mutex
    stopCh          chan struct{}
    wg              sync.WaitGroup
}
```

## How It Works
1. Initial assignment pass (same as ntm assign --auto)
2. Start completion detector in separate goroutine
3. Enter main loop:
   a. Wait for completion event on channel
   b. Lock assignment mutex
   c. Update assignment store
   d. Check for newly unblocked beads
   e. Assign to idle agents
   f. Unlock mutex
   g. Continue loop
4. Exit conditions:
   - User interrupt (Ctrl+C)
   - --stop-when-done and no more ready beads
   - All agents failed/crashed

## Concurrency Model
```
┌─────────────────────┐
│   Main Goroutine    │
│  (watch loop)       │
└─────────┬───────────┘
          │ reads from
          ▼
   completionCh
          ▲
          │ writes to
┌─────────┴───────────┐
│  Detector Goroutine │
│  (polls status,     │
│   matches patterns) │
└─────────────────────┘

Critical section: All assignment operations protected by assignmentLock
```

## Serialized Completion Handling
```go
func (w *WatchLoop) run(ctx context.Context) {
    for {
        select {
        case event := <-w.completionCh:
            w.assignmentLock.Lock()
            
            // 1. Update store
            w.store.MarkCompleted(event.BeadID)
            
            // 2. Log completion
            w.logCompletion(event)
            
            // 3. Check unblocked
            unblocked := w.depAssigner.GetNewlyUnblocked()
            
            // 4. Assign to idle
            idle := w.store.GetIdleAgents()
            w.assignNextBeads(unblocked, idle)
            
            w.assignmentLock.Unlock()
            
        case <-ctx.Done():
            return
        case <-w.stopCh:
            return
        }
    }
}
```

## Output (Streaming)
```
[12:34:01] Starting watch mode with strategy=balanced
[12:34:01] Initial assignment: 4 beads to 4 agents
[12:34:02]   bd-abc1 -> pane 2 (GreenCastle)
[12:34:02]   bd-def2 -> pane 3 (BlueLake)
[12:38:45] Completion: bd-abc1 by GreenCastle (4m 43s)
[12:38:45] Unblocked: bd-mno5, bd-pqr6
[12:38:46] Assigned: bd-mno5 -> pane 2 (GreenCastle)
...
[13:45:00] All beads complete. Exiting watch mode.
```

## Graceful Shutdown
```go
func (w *WatchLoop) Shutdown() {
    // 1. Signal stop
    close(w.stopCh)
    
    // 2. Wait for in-flight completions
    w.wg.Wait()
    
    // 3. Don't assign new work, but let current finish
    w.detector.Stop()
    
    // 4. Save state for resume
    w.store.Save()
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Watches for completion events
- [ ] Auto-assigns on completion
- [ ] Respects strategy flag
- [ ] --stop-when-done exits cleanly
- [ ] --delay controls assignment pacing
- [ ] Graceful shutdown on interrupt
- [ ] Streaming output with timestamps
- [ ] Handles all agents crashing
- [ ] Thread-safe completion handling
- [ ] No race conditions (passes go test -race)

## Tests Required
- Unit test: Watch loop logic
- Unit test: Exit conditions
- Unit test: Concurrent completions (10+ goroutines)
- E2E test: Full watch cycle with completions
- Race test: go test -race flag","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:54:43.182014392Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T03:42:44.791548718Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T03:42:44.791463197Z","close_reason":"Implementation verified complete:
- WatchLoop struct with concurrency-safe design (mutex, channels, WaitGroup)
- Run() method with main watch loop handling completion events
- handleCompletion() with serialized completion handling and auto-reassignment
- shouldStop() for --stop-when-done exit condition
- Graceful shutdown via signal handling (SIGINT/SIGTERM)
- All flags implemented: --watch, --stop-when-done, --delay, --watch-interval, --auto-reassign
- Initial assignment pass before entering watch loop
- Streaming output with timestamps via logf()
- Integration with CompletionDetector (pane loss, pattern matching, bead status polling)
- Race tests pass (go test -race)
- Unit tests tracked in bd-168bh","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3b4yu","depends_on_id":"bd-25cq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3b4yu","depends_on_id":"bd-264n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3b4yu","depends_on_id":"bd-k3zq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3b52y","title":"CAAM: Add --robot-account-status and --robot-accounts-list commands","description":"Add robot mode commands for account status and listing.

## Command: --robot-account-status

```bash
ntm --robot-account-status
ntm --robot-account-status --provider claude
```

### Output Schema

```json
{
  "accounts": {
    "claude": {
      "current": "account1@email.com",
      "usage_percent": 45,
      "limit_reset": "2024-01-15T12:00:00Z",
      "available_accounts": 3
    },
    "openai": {
      "current": "account2@email.com",
      "usage_percent": 80,
      "limit_reset": "2024-01-15T11:30:00Z",
      "available_accounts": 2
    }
  }
}
```

## Command: --robot-accounts-list

```bash
ntm --robot-accounts-list
ntm --robot-accounts-list --provider claude
```

### Output Schema

```json
{
  "accounts": [
    {
      "provider": "claude",
      "email": "account1@email.com",
      "current": true,
      "usage_percent": 45,
      "cooldown": null
    },
    {
      "provider": "claude",
      "email": "account2@email.com",
      "current": false,
      "usage_percent": 0,
      "cooldown": "2024-01-15T11:00:00Z"
    }
  ]
}
```

## Implementation
Both commands invoke caam with --json flag and transform output.

## Code Location

- internal/robot/accounts_list.go

 {"id":"bd-5z3e","title":"E2E Tests: Agent-to-agent handoff workflow","description":"E2E Tests for Agent-to-agent handoff workflow.

## Test Scenarios

1. Basic handoff
   - Spawn agent A, work on task partially
   - Kill agent A, spawn agent B with handoff
   - Verify context received
   - Verify B continues work

2. ntm handoff command
   - Run ntm handoff myproject cc_1 cc_2
   - Verify state captured from cc_1
   - Verify state injected to cc_2
   - Verify cc_2 acknowledges

3. Automatic handoff on crash
   - Enable auto-handoff
   - Simulate agent crash
   - Verify replacement spawned
   - Verify context transferred

4. Multi-hop handoff
   - Agent A -> Agent B -> Agent C
   - Verify context accumulates
   - Verify no info loss

## Detailed Logging
```
[E2E-HANDOFF] Capturing state from SOURCE: N bytes
[E2E-HANDOFF] State includes: N decisions, N files, N blockers
[E2E-HANDOFF] Injecting to TARGET
[E2E-HANDOFF] Target acknowledged handoff
```

## Acceptance Criteria

1. All 4 test scenarios implemented
2. Basic handoff preserves work context
3. ntm handoff command captures and injects state
4. Auto-handoff triggers on agent crash
5. Multi-hop preserves accumulated context
6. Detailed logging with [E2E-HANDOFF] prefix
7. Exit code 0 on all pass, non-zero on any failure","acceptance_criteria":"1. E2E tests verify agent-to-agent handoff
2. Handoff message delivered correctly
3. Context transferred between agents
4. Handoff logged in audit trail
5. Test cleanup removes test sessions
6.  {"id":"bd-64v8u","title":"RCH: Add dashboard widget for build offload status","description":"Add dashboard widget showing RCH build offload status.

## Widget Design

### Compact View
```
┌─ RCH Build Offload ─────────────┐
│ 🟢 45 builds (30 remote)        │
│ Time saved: ~20 min             │
└─────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Expanded View
```
┌─ RCH Status ─────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Workers:  builder-1 (🟢)  builder-2 (busy)       │
│                                                   │
│ Session Stats:                                    │
│   Total builds:     45                            │
│   Remote:           30 (67%)                      │
│   Local (fallback): 15 (33%)                      │
│   Time saved:       ~20 minutes                   │
│                                                   │
│ Current: cargo build --release on builder-2      │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Dashboard widget shows build offload status
2. Displays local vs remote build counts
3. Shows time saved via offloading
4. Worker health status visible
5. Real-time updates during builds
6. Unit tests for widget rendering
7. E2E test verifying accurate display","acceptance_criteria":"1. Dashboard widget shows build offload status
2. Displays local vs remote build counts
3. Shows time saved via offloading
4. Worker health status visible
5. Real-time updates during builds
6. Unit tests for widget rendering
7.  {"id":"bd-69cuc","title":"Task: Add TOON tool adapter (detect/version)","description":"## Overview
If a TOON CLI is used, add a tools adapter for detecting the `tru` binary and version.

## Details
- CLI is provided by toon_rust (binary: `tru`).
- Prefer `tru --version` for version parsing; fallback to `tru --help` if needed.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Detects installed `tru` binary
- Parses version output
- Used by `--robot-format=auto` to decide availability","notes":"UPDATE per bd-2csfi decision: This task is no longer needed.

 {"id":"bd-6fek","title":"Error Handling Tests: ntm init recovery scenarios","description":"Error Handling Tests for ntm init recovery scenarios.

## Error Scenarios to Test

1. Filesystem errors
   - Permission denied on directory creation
   - Disk full during config write
   - Read-only filesystem
   - Network filesystem latency

2. Config validation errors
   - Invalid TOML syntax recovery
   - Missing required fields
   - Type mismatches in config
   - Circular template references

3. Graceful degradation
   - Continue with defaults if config unreadable
   - Warn but proceed if optional dir creation fails
   - Fallback if flywheel tool unavailable

## Recovery Verification
- Test that partial init can be resumed
- Test that corrupted config is detected and reported
- Test cleanup on failure (no orphaned files)

## Logging Requirements
```
[ERROR] OPERATION: ERROR_MESSAGE
[RECOVER] Attempting: RECOVERY_ACTION
[CLEANUP] Removing: PATH
```

## Acceptance Criteria

1. All 3 error scenario categories tested
2. Filesystem errors handled gracefully
3. Config validation errors reported clearly
4. Graceful degradation falls back to defaults
5. Partial init can be resumed
6. Failed init cleans up orphaned files
7. Detailed logging with [ERROR]/[RECOVER]/[CLEANUP] prefixes","acceptance_criteria":"1. Error handling tests for ntm init
2. Recovery from partial init tested
3. Config file corruption handled
4. Permission errors handled
5. Test cleanup removes test configs
6. Test duration under 3 minutes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"EmeraldFinch","created_at":"2026-01-19T06:12:22.883192270Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T23:39:55.589828934Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T23:39:55.589762741Z","close_reason":"Added init recovery/error-handling tests (readonly target, corrupt config recovery, resume partial init, hooks degrade)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6fek","depends_on_id":"bd-38gl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:48:39Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-6fek","depends_on_id":"bd-ubhl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:48:39Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-6mls6","title":"Task: Implement 'ntm ensemble presets' command for preset management","description":"Implement `ntm ensemble presets` command to list available ensembles (built-in + user-defined).

## Command Specification
```
ntm ensemble presets [flags]
  --format string   Output format: table, json, yaml (default: table)
  --verbose         Show full ensemble configurations
```

- `ntm ensemble presets` is an alias for `ntm ensemble list` (shared implementation).
- Primary UX language: **ensemble**; “presets” is a convenience alias.

## Output Requirements
- Include `name`, `display`, `description`, `modes` (id + code), synthesis strategy, budget, tags
- Include `allow_advanced` flag when present
- In verbose mode, expand modes to include names, categories, and tiers

## Dependencies
- Built-in ensembles (bd-cph20)
- Ensemble loader (bd-1ovi8)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Lists built-in + user-defined ensembles
- [ ] JSON/YAML outputs are deterministic","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:39:28.550161191Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T20:36:42.595437577Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T20:36:42.595344604Z","close_reason":"Implemented ntm ensemble presets command with table/json/yaml formats, verbose mode, and tag filtering.  {"id":"bd-6qk8","title":"Task: Implement ntm level command","description":"## Overview
Create the 'ntm level' command for viewing and changing proficiency tier.

## Deliverables
1. Create internal/cli/level.go:
   - ntm level - show current tier and progress
   - ntm level up - promote to next tier
   - ntm level down - demote to previous tier
   - ntm level set <tier> - set specific tier (1, 2, 3 or apprentice, journeyman, master)

2. Output format for 'ntm level':
   Current proficiency: Journeyman (Tier 2)
   
   Usage stats:
     Commands run: 150
     Sessions created: 25
     Prompts sent: 200
     Using NTM since: 15 days
   
   Next tier: Master
     Unlocks: Robot mode, internal debugging, experimental features
     Suggested milestone: Run 50 more commands or 30 days of use
   
   Run 'ntm level master' to unlock all features now.

3. Promotion flow:
   - Confirm before tier change
   - Show what changes (new commands unlocked or hidden)
   - Log promotion to history

## Implementation Notes
- Use cobra subcommands for level operations
- Support both numeric (1,2,3) and named (apprentice, journeyman, master) tiers
- Add emoji support when terminal supports it

## Acceptance Criteria
- ntm level shows current status
- ntm level up/down work correctly
- Promotion history is recorded
- Unit tests for all subcommands","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:24:53.536644317Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T06:11:16.075203473Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T06:11:16.075154591Z","close_reason":"Implemented ntm level command in internal/cli/level.go with show/up/down/set subcommands, added to root.go and tiers registry","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6qk8","depends_on_id":"bd-1s9b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-6qk8","depends_on_id":"bd-7ny3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"}]}

+func TestCheckAllAgents_Disabled(t *testing.T) {
+	config := DefaultCompactionTriggerConfig()
+	config.AutoCompact = false // Disable auto-compact
+
+	monitor := NewContextMonitor(DefaultMonitorConfig())
+	compactor := NewCompactor(monitor, DefaultCompactorConfig())
+	predictor := NewContextPredictor(DefaultPredictorConfig())
+
+	trigger := NewCompactionTrigger(config, monitor, compactor, predictor)
+
+	// Register an agent with high usage
+	monitor.RegisterAgent("agent-1", "pane-1", "claude-opus-4")
+	state := monitor.GetState("agent-1")
+	state.cumulativeInputTokens = 150000
+	state.cumulativeOutputTokens = 150000
+
+	// checkAllAgents should return immediately due to disabled AutoCompact
+	trigger.checkAllAgents() // Should not panic or attempt compaction
+}
+
+func TestCheckAllAgents_NoAgents(t *testing.T) {
+	config := DefaultCompactionTriggerConfig()
+	config.AutoCompact = true
+
+	monitor := NewContextMonitor(DefaultMonitorConfig())
+	compactor := NewCompactor(monitor, DefaultCompactorConfig())
+	predictor := NewContextPredictor(DefaultPredictorConfig())
+
+	trigger := NewCompactionTrigger(config, monitor, compactor, predictor)
+
+	// No agents registered — should do nothing
+	trigger.checkAllAgents() // Should not panic
+}
+
